input.headline: NASA unveils close-up pictures of the comet popping by from another star

raw.headline: NASA unveils close-up pictures of the comet popping by from another star

winner: I guess that comet was a little *interstellar* with its visit!

candidates.text: Why did the comet get a ticket? It was speeding through the solar system!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is simple and uses a common pun ('speeding'), but lacks specificity to the headline's context (NASA, comet from another star). It’s mildly funny but not particularly surprising or tailored to the situation."}

candidates.text: NASA says the comet just popped by, leaving no forwarding address.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'popped by' and 'no forwarding address' are clever and specific, aligning well with the headline's context."}

candidates.text: Astronomers were thrilled when they got a glimpse of that passing comet—turns out, it was just a space rock with a really expensive viewfinder.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific, but the pun on 'viewfinder' feels a bit forced and less natural. It also doesn't fully tie into the comet's origin from another star as mentioned in the headline."}

candidates.text: I guess that comet was a little *interstellar* with its visit!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke uses a play on 'interstellar' which relates to the comet's origin, but the humor is somewhat generic and the pun is mild. It meets the constraints but lacks stronger surprise or specificity."}

candidates.text: NASA released new photos of the comet passing by Earth and it’s actually kind of disappointing—it doesn’t even have a name yet.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific, but the humor is weak and relies on a minor disappointment that may not resonate strongly. The explanation feels generic and lacks punch."}
